Purpose: Allows the robot to connect to a wireless network so it can be monitored and controlled remotely
Stores the robot's operating system software and recorded data from the system
Provides portable power for the Raspberry Pi so the robot can run without being plugged into a wall outlet
Used during setup to connect the Raspberry Pi to a display for configuration and debugging
Moves mechanical parts of the robot such as arms or the camera mount
Acts as the interface between the controller and motors allowing speed and direction control
Drives the robot wheels and provides the movement needed for navigation
Measures distance to objects so the robot can avoid collisions
Captures video and images and helps the robot see in low light conditions
Works as the main computer running the robot software and voice command system
Handles low level hardware control such as motors and sensors
